info.name: MENTORA LMG Service - Complete API
info.description: Complete Postman collection for the Learning Material Generator Service including n8n webhook callbacks. Covers health checks, mastery profiles, learning materials, agent monitoring, and n8n webhook integration.

variable.description: Paste the access_token from login response here. Do not add 'Bearer ' prefix.

body.raw:
{
    "student_id": "{{studentId}}",
    "analysis_timestamp": "2026-03-18T14:30:00Z",
    "data_sources": {
        "github": "available",
        "sandbox": "available",
        "quizzes": "available"
    },
    "mastery_profile": {
        "overall_mastery_score": 58,
        "knowledge_gaps": [
            {
                "topic": "Recursion",
                "topic_id": "CS101-REC",
                "gap_type": "FUNDAMENTAL_GAP",
                "confidence": 0.95,
                "misconceptions": [
                    "base case identification",
                    "stack frame management",
                    "recursive vs iterative thinking"
                ],
                "observed_error_patterns": {
                    "sandbox": [
                        "stack overflow errors",
                        "infinite recursion",
                        "incorrect return values"
                    ],
                    "quizzes": [
                        "cannot identify base case in multiple choice",
                        "fails to trace recursive calls"
                    ],
                    "github": [
                        "Big Bang commit with perfect recursive functions (no intermediate attempts)",
                        "no debugging history or incremental improvements"
                    ]
                },
                "evidence_summary": "GitHub shows a single massive commit introducing perfect recursive functions (AI probability 87%). Sandbox tasks on recursion had 0% success rate with extremely fast completion times (45s), suggesting copy-paste without understanding. Quiz scores on recursion topics are 20%, with specific misconceptions on base cases and stack frames.",
                "prerequisite_topics": ["Loops", "Functions", "Call Stack"],
                "related_topics": ["Tree Traversal", "Divide and Conquer"],
                "suggested_intervention": {
                    "primary": "interactive_tutorial",
                    "secondary": ["practice_exercises", "peer_tutoring"],
                    "difficulty_level": "beginner",
                    "estimated_time_minutes": 90,
                    "learning_objectives": [
                        "Define recursion and identify its components",
                        "Write recursive functions with proper base cases",
                        "Trace recursive calls and understand stack frames",
                        "Convert between recursive and iterative solutions"
                    ]
                }
            },
            {
                "topic": "Binary Search Trees",
                "topic_id": "CS201-BST",
                "gap_type": "FUNDAMENTAL_GAP",
                "confidence": 0.98,
                "misconceptions": [
                    "BST deletion algorithm (all cases)",
                    "tree traversal order (inorder, preorder, postorder)",
                    "balancing concepts"
                ],
                "observed_error_patterns": {
                    "sandbox": [
                        "deletion implementation fails for nodes with two children",
                        "incorrect traversal output",
                        "no attempt at balancing"
                    ],
                    "quizzes": [
                        "cannot explain deletion steps",
                        "confuses traversal orders"
                    ],
                    "github": [
                        "Big Bang commit (+350 lines) with complete BST implementation (AI probability 94%)",
                        "no prior commits or incremental development",
                        "code is perfectly formatted with no errors"
                    ]
                },
                "evidence_summary": "GitHub analysis reveals a single massive commit containing a fully functional BST (AI probability 94%). Sandbox attempts show 100% success but with suspiciously fast completion (30s) – likely copy-paste. Quiz scores are 15%, with major misconceptions on deletion and traversals. Student clearly does not understand BSTs despite submitting correct code.",
                "prerequisite_topics": ["Recursion", "Trees", "Pointers/References"],
                "related_topics": ["AVL Trees", "Hash Tables"],
                "suggested_intervention": {
                    "primary": "step_by_step_practice",
                    "secondary": ["video_tutorial", "code_review_with_peer"],
                    "difficulty_level": "intermediate",
                    "estimated_time_minutes": 120,
                    "learning_objectives": [
                        "Understand BST properties and structure",
                        "Implement BST insertion and search",
                        "Master BST deletion for all three cases",
                        "Perform inorder, preorder, and postorder traversals"
                    ]
                }
            },
            {
                "topic": "Exception Handling",
                "topic_id": "CS102-EXC",
                "gap_type": "PARTIAL_GAP",
                "confidence": 0.70,
                "misconceptions": [
                    "checked vs unchecked exceptions",
                    "try-catch-finally flow",
                    "when to use custom exceptions"
                ],
                "observed_error_patterns": {
                    "sandbox": [
                        "incorrect exception type caught",
                        "finally block not used appropriately"
                    ],
                    "quizzes": [
                        "confuses checked and unchecked exceptions"
                    ],
                    "github": [
                        "exception handling appears fully formed in commits (medium risk)",
                        "some refactoring exists, but no evolution from basic to advanced"
                    ]
                },
                "evidence_summary": "GitHub shows exception handling code appearing relatively complete without clear evolution (medium risk). Sandbox success rate 67% with errors on exception types. Quiz score 60% with specific misconceptions on checked/unchecked. This is a partial gap that can be closed with targeted practice.",
                "prerequisite_topics": ["Control Flow", "Methods"],
                "related_topics": ["File I/O", "Logging"],
                "suggested_intervention": {
                    "primary": "targeted_quiz",
                    "secondary": ["code_examples", "debugging_exercise"],
                    "difficulty_level": "beginner",
                    "estimated_time_minutes": 45,
                    "learning_objectives": [
                        "Differentiate checked and unchecked exceptions",
                        "Use try-catch-finally blocks correctly",
                        "Create and throw custom exceptions"
                    ]
                }
            }
        ],
        "strengths": [
            {
                "topic": "Loops",
                "topic_id": "CS101-LOOP",
                "confidence": 0.96,
                "mastery_level": "advanced",
                "evidence_summary": "GitHub shows incremental commits with messages like 'fixed off-by-one error' (low risk). Sandbox 100% success with reasonable times and error patterns indicating learning. Quiz score 95% with no misconceptions.",
                "can_teach_others": true
            },
            {
                "topic": "File I/O",
                "topic_id": "CS102-FILE",
                "confidence": 0.94,
                "mastery_level": "proficient",
                "evidence_summary": "GitHub low risk, incremental commits adding error handling over time. Sandbox consistent performance. Quiz not tested but GitHub evidence strong.",
                "can_teach_others": false
            }
        ]
    },
    "recommendations": {
        "priority_order": ["Binary Search Trees", "Recursion", "Exception Handling"],
        "general_advice": "Focus on fundamental gaps first (BST and Recursion) as they are prerequisites for many advanced topics. Use interactive tutorials and peer tutoring. For Exception Handling, targeted quizzes and examples should suffice.",
        "for_instructor": "Student may be using AI for difficult topics. Verify understanding with proctored tasks. Encourage incremental commits and better commit messages."
    }
}

exec: pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function() {
pm.test('Returns new job_id on retry', function() {

item.name: n8n Webhooks
item.description: Webhook endpoints for n8n to call back with generated materials and job updates. Requires X-Webhook-Secret header.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/material
item.description: Receive a single generated material from n8n workflow. Saves to MongoDB and updates job tracking.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/material - Missing Secret (401)
item.description: Test that requests without X-Webhook-Secret are rejected.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/material - Wrong Secret (403)
item.description: Test that requests with invalid X-Webhook-Secret are rejected.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/material/batch
item.description: Receive multiple generated materials from n8n in a single callback.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/job/status
item.description: Update job status from n8n workflow (progress, completion, or failure).

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/job/status - Processing
item.description: Send a processing/in-progress status update from n8n.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/job/status - Failed
item.description: Send a failed status update from n8n with error details.

item.name: POST /api/webhooks/n8n/mastery
item.description: Callback from n8n confirming mastery profile processing has started.
